## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright guys, so today I'm going to take you through messenger ads. Now this has changed quite a bit in the last few months alone and there's some really exciting stuff that you can do here. There's really two ways to effectively use messenger ads. The first way is where you can send people to messenger so you can get people to message you or your page. And this is effectively any normal ad on Facebook. It looks the same. It appears on desktop or mobile. But instead of linking through to a website, it links through to your messenger inbox. And on the back of that you can have all sorts of bots and responses and all of that good stuff. But the first way is to essentially send traffic to messenger. And the second way is to actually show your ads inside of messenger. So the first way is you're sending traffic to messenger. The second way is you're actually showing ads inside of messenger. Why would you want to show ads inside of messenger? Well Facebook has put a lot of time and effort into building up the audience user base of messenger and there's now 1.2 billion people on the planet that use messenger. So it's a massive piece of real estate inventory or advertising inventory that has just opened up for advertisers. So let's take a look at what this looks like or these two different options inside of the ad account before I actually take you in an ad account and show you on the back end. So the first one on the top is messenger as a placement. So when you edit your placements or you select your placements at your ad set level, you'll see you'll have Facebook, Instagram, audience network, and then you'll see you have messenger at the bottom. Now messenger as a placement is where this shows up inside of messenger. Makes sense right? Your ad is placed in messenger. On the bottom however what we have is messenger as a link. So this is where we're sending our traffic. So Facebook again at the ad set level says to you, where do you want to send your traffic? A website, an app, or messenger. So this means that your ad is essentially sending that traffic to messenger instead of an external website or a landing page or whatever that might be. Now if we're looking at the placement, the objectives that you can select for that is conversion objective, traffic objective, and app install objectives. So those are the three campaign objectives you can use to have messenger as a placement. And on the right hand side there you'll see an example of what that ad looks like inside
